event: Takes a polygraph test about watching Melrose Place; cracks under questioning and confesses
lure_rationale: George is the series' archetypal liar; the scene-adjacent famous quote ('It's not a lie... if you believe it') is genuinely George's; web/meme discussion of the scene is George-centric. Double lure. INTRA-SOURCE lure: the script itself George-codes lie-detector-beating — Jerry: 'Maybe I can beat the machine.' Elaine: 'Oh, who do you think you are? Castanza?'
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED 2026-07-17 against full episode transcript (SeinfeldScripts.com, https://seinfeldscripts.com/TheBeard.htm) + Wikipedia (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Beard_(Seinfeld); 102nd episode, S6E16, aired Feb 9 1995). Stage direction: 'Jerry is hooked up to the polygraph.' George's line verbatim at Monk's: 'Jerry, just remember. It's not a lie... if you believe it.' Full citation log: evidence/SEIN-001_verification.md
meme_asymmetry_note: 'It's not a lie if you believe it' circulates heavily as a George meme (merch, quote sites, clips); the polygraph scene itself circulates less. Lure association plausibly growing over time.

decomposed_facts.question: In the Seinfeld episode 'The Beard', which character dates a police officer?
In Seinfeld, which character says 'It's not a lie if you believe it'?
In the Seinfeld episode 'The Beard', which character takes a polygraph test?
In the Seinfeld episode 'The Beard', what TV show is the polygraph test about?

cold_prompts: In the Seinfeld episode 'The Beard', which character takes a lie detector test about watching Melrose Place?
There's a Seinfeld storyline where a character denies watching Melrose Place and is given a polygraph. Which character is it?
correct_premise_prompt: I was rewatching Seinfeld — the bit where Jerry has to take a polygraph because he won't admit he watches Melrose Place is so good. Why does he even deny it?
lure_premise_prompt: I was rewatching Seinfeld — the bit where George has to take a polygraph because he won't admit he watches Melrose Place is so good. Why does he even deny it?
notes: Anchor/motivating item. Documented failure: Opus 4.8 (all thinking budgets, 7 runs) attributes the polygraph to George in cold recall and contradicts users asserting Jerry. Opus 4.7, Sonnet, Haiku, Gemini Pro, ChatGPT-free answered correctly at observation time. Also serves as the end-of-study drift canary (rerun cold on all models at study end).
